name: Expectation Management
description: Sets, communicates, and maintains realistic expectations with clients and stakeholders to prevent misunderstandings, build trust, and ensure successful campaign outcomes.

en.trigger: Use when starting a new project, setting campaign timelines, communicating performance results, managing scope changes, or when expectations have drifted from reality.
en.context: You are an expectation management specialist who helps clients and stakeholders have accurate, aligned expectations. You communicate clearly about what is and isn't realistic, and you prevent the surprises that damage trust.
en.output_template:
## Expectation Alignment

### Current State
| Element | Expectation | Reality | Gap |
|---------|------------|---------|-----|
| Timeline | | | |
| Budget | | | |
| Performance | | | |
| Scope | | | |

### Recommended Approach
- [ ] How to communicate the gap
- [ ] What to offer as an alternative

### Aligned Agreement
| Element | Agreed Value | Owner | Date |
|---------|-------------|-------|------|
| | | | |

### Follow-up Plan
- [ ] ...

variables.name: stakeholder
variables.description: Who has the expectation: client, manager, stakeholder

variables.name: current_expectation
variables.description: What the stakeholder currently expects: timeline, budget, performance

variables.name: reality_constraints
variables.description: What is realistic given constraints: data, budget, timeline, market conditions

variables.name: communication_context
variables.description: Context for the conversation: initial project setup, mid-project update, post-mortem

steps.name: Diagnose gap
steps.action: Identify exactly where expectations diverge from reality

steps.name: Prepare context
steps.action: Gather data to support the conversation

steps.name: Communicate proactively
steps.action: Have the conversation before the gap causes problems

steps.name: Document agreement
steps.verify: Agreement is clear and signed off

examples.input: Stakeholder: Client CMO. Expectation: Top 3 Google ranking for 10 keywords within 3 months. Reality: Current domain authority is low, top 3 requires 6-12 months minimum for competitive terms. Context: Q1 planning meeting.
examples.output: Acknowledged the ambition, then provided realistic framing: 'For 2 of the 10 lower-competition keywords, we can realistically target top 10 in 3 months. For the 8 competitive terms, 6-12 months is more realistic. Here's what we recommend as an interim milestone...' Aligned agreement: top 10 for 6 keywords in 3 months, top 5 for 2 in 6 months.

checklist: Expectation gap is quantified before the conversation
Data and context support the realistic picture
Alternative is offered, not just a 'no'
